content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all changes across src/adapters/fs.rs and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs. The implementation correctly addresses all three issues: (1) deny_unknown_fields + round-trip tests catch journal struct drift, (2) runless-to-named completion now accepts when started_at matches and rejects on mismatch, (3) plan_hash auto-population from snapshot only on new entry creation, with intentional skip on existing row backfill/finalization to prevent plan-evolution mislabeling. All 1163 tests pass. Test coverage is thorough, including plan-evolution edge cases, legacy-row preservation, and terminal replay safety. Two minor suggestions: snapshot_plan_hash silently swallows non-NotFound I/O errors, and the enforcement strategy for struct sync could be documented more explicitly.

## Amendments

1. snapshot_plan_hash() silently swallows I/O errors (corrupt snapshot JSON, permission failures) via .ok(), making them indistinguishable from 'no snapshot exists.' Consider logging a warning or tracing event when read_snapshot returns an Err that is not NotFound, so operators can detect a corrupted snapshot that is silently preventing plan_hash auto-population.
   Rationale: A corrupt snapshot file would cause plan_hash to silently remain None on every new task run entry, with no signal in logs. This is a degraded-data-quality issue rather than a correctness bug, but it undermines the goal of Issue 3 (lineage should not lose plan linkage) because the system would fail to auto-populate without any indication of why.
2. The deny_unknown_fields guard on StartJournalDetails and CompletionJournalDetails provides runtime detection (parse returns None, which causes journal repair to fall back to appending), but the stated acceptance criterion asks for 'compile-time or test failure.' The round-trip tests fulfill the 'test failure' criterion only if they are run — consider adding a doc-comment or CLAUDE.md note explaining that the deny_unknown_fields + round-trip test pair is the chosen enforcement mechanism, so future developers know this is an intentional two-layer guard rather than just a serde annotation.
   Rationale: Without this documentation, a future developer who adds a field to the model's payload struct but forgets to run tests could ship silently drifted structs. The deny_unknown_fields provides a runtime safety net (parse fails, repair skips), but the stated goal is compile-time or test detection. Making the enforcement strategy explicit reduces the chance of someone relying on only one layer.
